markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' confirmed November 19, 2026 release for Grand Theft Auto VI on PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X/S—following delays from a Fall 2025 window to May 2026 and then November—anchors trader sentiment around what major Rockstar milestones or external events could still unfold beforehand. Take-Two Interactive reaffirmed the date during its May 2026 earnings call and signaled that marketing will intensify this summer, potentially including new trailers or promotional pushes that historically precede big launches. Industry watchers note ongoing final development crunch, including reports of extended QA hours, alongside continued GTA Online updates that could serve as the last major support before the new title arrives. These factors, combined with the game's massive cultural anticipation and Rockstar's emphasis on polish over rushed timelines, shape expectations for any pre-release announcements or competitive releases in the coming months.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Harvey Weinstein’s advanced age, documented health issues including cancer, and the timeline of his New York retrials are the main drivers behind the market’s heavy weighting toward no additional prison time.** The 74-year-old producer has already served multiple years while facing consecutive sentencing from his upheld California conviction, making further meaningful incarceration less likely if appeals or plea negotiations succeed. Recent split verdicts and mistrials in the 2025–2026 Manhattan proceedings on remaining charges have created openings for time-served resolutions or lighter outcomes, especially given the shorter potential penalties on the unresolved rape count. Traders appear to view the combination of his physical condition, ongoing legal maneuvering, and the practical limits of consecutive terms as outweighing the risk of substantially longer sentences.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold an 89% implied probability in the 2026 Stanley Cup market due to their dominant 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights in the Finals, combined with superior regular-season form that produced 113 points and the Eastern Conference's top record. The Canes' depth, strong home-ice advantage at Lenovo Center, and consistent playoff execution have driven trader consensus, especially after securing Game 5 by a 4-2 margin. Vegas's path included a sweep of the Avalanche but faces steeper challenges against Carolina's structure and goaltending tandem. Outcomes could still shift with a Golden Knights rally in Game 6 or 7, significant injuries to key Carolina skaters, or unexpected goaltending swings in high-stakes elimination scenarios.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit neck-and-neck as the narrow leaders in the 2026 World Cup winner market because both enter with proven recent pedigree—Spain’s Euro 2024 triumph and deep young core featuring Lamine Yamal, and France’s back-to-back final appearances built around Kylian Mbappé and elite depth. Portugal, England, Argentina, and Brazil follow closely, each carrying star power, strong FIFA rankings, and favorable group-stage starts that keep implied probabilities bunched. Early tournament results, including strong host-nation performances and mixed outcomes for Brazil and the Netherlands, have reinforced the sense that no single side has separated itself in a 48-team field where form, fitness, and knockout matchups can shift rapidly.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military as of mid-2026, with no designated successor promoted to the Politburo Standing Committee and leadership discussions focused on a potential fourth term at the 2027 Party Congress rather than any transition. Recent developments reinforcing this include ongoing anti-corruption purges targeting senior military figures such as Zhang Youxia, which have further centralized authority under Xi, alongside active diplomacy including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and a June visit to North Korea. Term limits on the presidency were eliminated in 2018, enabling his precedent-breaking third term, and analyses indicate the absence of visible internal challenges or health constraints supports trader consensus around continuity through 2027.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading share in this wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market due to his high national visibility as California governor, frequent media positioning against the current administration, and early moves such as hiring consumer-focused regulators and engaging on issues like AI regulation. Recent polls show a fragmented field often led by name recognition, with Kamala Harris competitive or ahead in some surveys and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez gaining ground through national outreach and progressive visibility. Jon Ossoff and others trail on Senate records or regional appeal. Key differentiators include fundraising capacity, electability signals among swing voters and key blocs such as Black voters in Southern states, and ideological positioning amid party discussions favoring moderation. Consolidation could occur after the 2026 midterms, through formal candidacies, or via shifts in early polling and endorsements ahead of primary contests.
